title: Pan-Seared Fish Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew made with fresh fish and a variety of savory spices. Perfect for a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: fish
ingredients.notes: Any firm white fish such as halibut or cod works well.

ingredients.name: carrot
ingredients.notes: Peel and chop the carrots into bite-sized pieces.

ingredients.name: potato
ingredients.notes: Peel and chop the potatoes into bite-sized pieces.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion into small pieces.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds a lot of flavor to the stew.

ingredients.name: stew
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality fish stew mix for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: norwegian
ingredients.notes: A pinch of Norwegian seasoning adds a unique flavor to the stew.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium broth for a healthier option.

instructions: Heat the water in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and Norwegian seasoning to the pot and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the fish, carrot, and potato to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the stew mix and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
